title: mentha linnaei var. germanica
intro: Mentha linnaei var. germanica, also known as German mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family and native to Europe. Its leaves and stems contain menthol, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid. German mint has been used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as nausea and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as to soothe headaches and colds. German mint can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture for its medicinal benefits, and is often used in traditional medicine and cooking.
